_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index of average prices for coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice based on 2015 levels set to 100, currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since 2015. This rise indicates a notable yet uneven climb across commodities within the basket. The increase has been driven unevenly over the years, with some items seeing sharper gains recently than others. This composite index helps track broad trends in key soft commodities used in breakfast foods and drinks.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged 93% since 2015, substantially outpacing the other components. Orange juice has also increased by 22%, while wheat and sugar have seen modest gains of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price movements primarily reflect supply shocks caused by weather disruptions and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee and citrus greening impacting orange juice. The markets for these soft commodities are relatively thin, meaning smaller changes in supply can cause disproportionate price swings rather than broad inflationary trends.
notes.why: The breakfast basket's rise is largely driven by a few weather- and disease-sensitive crops, indicating localized rather than systemic inflationary pressure. Because of the vulnerability of these crops to environmental factors, elevated prices may persist over several years. This underscores the importance of weather-related risks in shaping commodity price dynamics in thin markets.
